title: castilleja exserta subsp. latifolia
intro: Castilleja exserta subsp. latifolia, commonly known as Indian paintbrush, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, deriving some of its nutrients from other plants, and is native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. Indian paintbrush can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as to promote overall well-being and immune function.
